title: List, read and search

publication.changeSummary: Topic-specific draft for List, read and search; requires human source, technical, pedagogy, and accessibility review. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: Pro Git; second edition online; section applicable to List, read and search
sources.claim: Read-only inspection commands establish location, directory contents, file content, status, and differences before any command changes filesystem or repository state.

sources.locator: CS2023 Software Engineering; August 2023; section applicable to List, read and search
sources.claim: Read-only inspection commands establish location, directory contents, file content, status, and differences before any command changes filesystem or repository state.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Read-only inspection commands establish location, directory contents, file content, status, and differences before any command changes filesystem or repository state.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: Before editing, a learner prints the working directory, lists hidden entries, checks Git status, and opens the exact target file. Your goal is to locate a configuration or symbol in a project without modifying files. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: pf tools files; Paths and project trees. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Operational model
sections.body: Read-only inspection commands establish location, directory contents, file content, status, and differences before any command changes filesystem or repository state. The learner should use this model to predict an observable result rather than merely repeat terminology.

sections.heading: Boundary and failure point
sections.body: Hidden files, ignored files, permissions, binary content, pager behavior, and large output can make one inspection command incomplete. A correct explanation therefore includes the applicable assumptions and avoids this common mistake: Inspection is unnecessary because command prompts and editor tabs always identify the active directory and file accurately.

includes: List hidden files, inspect text and search names or content with bounded commands.
Locate a configuration or symbol in a project without modifying files.
excludes: Hidden files, ignored files, permissions, binary content, pager behavior, and large output can make one inspection command incomplete.

outcomes: Locate a configuration or symbol in a project without modifying files.

examples.title: List, read and search: direct case
examples.situation: Before editing, a learner prints the working directory, lists hidden entries, checks Git status, and opens the exact target file.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the starting evidence: Before editing, a learner prints the working directory, lists hidden entries, checks Git status, and opens the exact target file.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Check the result against this correction: Collect cheap read-only evidence before mutation so later commands operate on the intended path, file, branch, and repository state.
examples.result: The case supports the declared outcome because Collect cheap read-only evidence before mutation so later commands operate on the intended path, file, branch, and repository state.

examples.title: List, read and search: boundary case
examples.situation: After a surprising build failure, file metadata and a diff reveal that the edited file is a similarly named copy outside the repository.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Identify the changed context: After a surprising build failure, file metadata and a diff reveal that the edited file is a similarly named copy outside the repository.
Step 2 — Keep this boundary explicit: Hidden files, ignored files, permissions, binary content, pager behavior, and large output can make one inspection command incomplete.
Step 3 — Reject the documented misconception: Inspection is unnecessary because command prompts and editor tabs always identify the active directory and file accurately.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Locate a configuration or symbol in a project without modifying files.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: Inspection is unnecessary because command prompts and editor tabs always identify the active directory and file accurately.
misconceptions.correction: Collect cheap read-only evidence before mutation so later commands operate on the intended path, file, branch, and repository state.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Using the direct and boundary scenarios, explain why this belief fails: Inspection is unnecessary because command prompts and editor tabs always identify the active directory and file accurately.

analogy.example: A labeled filing room can model List, read and search: current location changes how relative directions resolve, and moving differs from copying a folder.
usefulFor: Reasoning about exact locations, inspection, file operations, and destructive scope
limitations: Shell expansion, permissions, links, filesystem rules, and command execution can behave unlike physical folders.
Hidden files, ignored files, permissions, binary content, pager behavior, and large output can make one inspection command incomplete.

recap.summary: You should now be able to locate a configuration or symbol in a project without modifying files. Keep this boundary in view: Hidden files, ignored files, permissions, binary content, pager behavior, and large output can make one inspection command incomplete.
retrievalPrompts: Explain List, read and search without using the optional analogy and include one observable consequence.
Why is this statement incorrect: Inspection is unnecessary because command prompts and editor tabs always identify the active directory and file accurately.
Apply the model to this case from memory: After a surprising build failure, file metadata and a diff reveal that the edited file is a similarly named copy outside the repository.
recap.nextReviewPrompt: Next: continue to “Create, copy, move and rename”. Before moving on, answer one retrieval prompt without reopening the explanation.
